description: Shailesh Vara was Secretary of State for Northern Ireland between 7 July 2022 and 6 September 2022. He previously served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State and then Minister of State at the Northern Ireland Office between January 2018 and November 2018. He served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Work and Pensions from July 2015 until July 2016 and at the Ministry of Justice from October 2013 until July 2016. He served as an Assistant Government Whip (HM Treasury) from May 2010 until September 2012. He was elected Conservative MP for North West Cambridgeshire in 2005. Education Shailesh was educated at Aylesbury Grammar School and Brunel University and qualified as a solicitor. Career He has served as a member of the Select Committee on Environment, Food and Rural Affairs and during 2006 was Joint Secretary of the Conservative Backbench Group on Foreign affairs and became a Government Whip in 2010. Personal life Shailesh is married with two children.

details.body:
Shailesh Vara was Secretary of State for Northern Ireland between 7 July 2022 and 6 September 2022.

He previously served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State and then Minister of State at the Northern Ireland Office between January 2018 and November 2018. He served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Work and Pensions from July 2015 until July 2016 and at the Ministry of Justice from October 2013 until July 2016. He served as an Assistant Government Whip (HM Treasury) from May 2010 until September 2012. He was elected Conservative MP for North West Cambridgeshire in 2005.

Education

Shailesh was educated at Aylesbury Grammar School and Brunel University and qualified as a solicitor.

Career

He has served as a member of the Select Committee on Environment, Food and Rural Affairs and during 2006 was Joint Secretary of the Conservative Backbench Group on Foreign affairs and became a Government Whip in 2010.

Personal life

Shailesh is married with two children.

Shailesh Vara was Secretary of State for Northern Ireland between 7 July 2022 and 6 September 2022.

He previously served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State and then Minister of State at the Northern Ireland Office between January 2018 and November 2018. He served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Work and Pensions from July 2015 until July 2016 and at the Ministry of Justice from October 2013 until July 2016. He served as an Assistant Government Whip (HM Treasury) from May 2010 until September 2012. He was elected Conservative MP for North West Cambridgeshire in 2005.

Education

Shailesh was educated at Aylesbury Grammar School and Brunel University and qualified as a solicitor.

Career

He has served as a member of the Select Committee on Environment, Food and Rural Affairs and during 2006 was Joint Secretary of the Conservative Backbench Group on Foreign affairs and became a Government Whip in 2010.

Personal life

Shailesh is married with two children.

The Secretary of State has overall responsibility for the ministerial work of the Northern Ireland Office.

The Secretary of State leads on:

  • Engagement with the NI Executive (focusing on the Executive Office, Department of Justice, Department of Finance and Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs);
  • Political stability;
  • Relationships with other governments;
  • Implementation of the Windsor Framework;
  • Legacy;
  • National security;
  • Work to ensure that NI is fully engaged in the development and delivery of new government policy, especially the Missions-led approach.

He also has overall responsibility for the NIO’s parliamentary activity.

role.title: Secretary of State for Northern Ireland

role_appointments.title: Shailesh Vara MP - Secretary of State for Northern Ireland

details.body:
Shailesh Vara was Secretary of State for Northern Ireland between 7 July 2022 and 6 September 2022.

He previously served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State and then Minister of State at the Northern Ireland Office between January 2018 and November 2018. He served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Work and Pensions from July 2015 until July 2016 and at the Ministry of Justice from October 2013 until July 2016. He served as an Assistant Government Whip (HM Treasury) from May 2010 until September 2012. He was elected Conservative MP for North West Cambridgeshire in 2005.

Education

Shailesh was educated at Aylesbury Grammar School and Brunel University and qualified as a solicitor.

Career

He has served as a member of the Select Committee on Environment, Food and Rural Affairs and during 2006 was Joint Secretary of the Conservative Backbench Group on Foreign affairs and became a Government Whip in 2010.

Personal life

Shailesh is married with two children.

The Northern Ireland Office is responsible for overseeing the Northern Ireland devolution settlement and representing Northern Ireland interests at UK government level and UK government interests in Northern Ireland.

The Minister of State reports to the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland.

Responsibilities are:

  • Brexit

  • Security-related issues and casework

  • Legacy stakeholder management

  • Parliamentary liaison with Northern Ireland MPs the Northern Ireland Affairs Committee, and other interested parliamentarians

Shailesh Vara was Secretary of State for Northern Ireland between 7 July 2022 and 6 September 2022.

He previously served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State and then Minister of State at the Northern Ireland Office between January 2018 and November 2018. He served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Work and Pensions from July 2015 until July 2016 and at the Ministry of Justice from October 2013 until July 2016. He served as an Assistant Government Whip (HM Treasury) from May 2010 until September 2012. He was elected Conservative MP for North West Cambridgeshire in 2005.

Education

Shailesh was educated at Aylesbury Grammar School and Brunel University and qualified as a solicitor.

Career

He has served as a member of the Select Committee on Environment, Food and Rural Affairs and during 2006 was Joint Secretary of the Conservative Backbench Group on Foreign affairs and became a Government Whip in 2010.

Personal life

Shailesh is married with two children.

The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • benefit cap implementation and benefit uprating
  • other social assistance, including housing, supported accommodation, Support for Mortgage Interest, Cold Weather Payments, Bereavement Benefit and funeral payments
  • child maintenance and relationship support
  • cross-DWP House of Commons spokesperson

role.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Welfare Delivery

details.body:
Shailesh Vara was Secretary of State for Northern Ireland between 7 July 2022 and 6 September 2022.

He previously served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State and then Minister of State at the Northern Ireland Office between January 2018 and November 2018. He served as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Work and Pensions from July 2015 until July 2016 and at the Ministry of Justice from October 2013 until July 2016. He served as an Assistant Government Whip (HM Treasury) from May 2010 until September 2012. He was elected Conservative MP for North West Cambridgeshire in 2005.

Education

Shailesh was educated at Aylesbury Grammar School and Brunel University and qualified as a solicitor.

Career

He has served as a member of the Select Committee on Environment, Food and Rural Affairs and during 2006 was Joint Secretary of the Conservative Backbench Group on Foreign affairs and became a Government Whip in 2010.

Personal life

Shailesh is married with two children.

Responsibilities include:

  • courts and tribunals (including HMCTS and fees/income)
  • administrative justice
  • legal aid (including the Legal Aid Agency)
  • legal services (including the Legal Services Board)
  • better regulation and growth
  • sustainability
  • judicial diversity
